## prompt-final-framework

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-adoption.md`
(SUB-PROMPT SCHEDULE item 4), adapted from the prior build's
`review/sub-prompts/prompt-4-final-narrative.md` and the final-paper polish of
`review/final-narrative`. This build is analogous to, and does not rewrite, the
prior review.*

Write the final-framework into `law-physical-ai-trials/adoption/final-framework/`
by carrying the full-framework to publication quality. Read the full-framework
critically, list the corrections it needs, and implement them here: this is the
self-learning step in which the lessons identified from the full stage are
applied. The final manuscript is the submission-ready version a senior author
would sign.

**Corrections to identify and apply (senior-author finishing).**

- White space: remove large empty bands; where interword spacing looks uneven,
  tune `\RaggedRight`, `\emergencystretch`, and `\tolerance` so words are evenly
  spaced with no right-margin overflow.
- Pagination: no orphan or widow line, no one or two word last line, no single
  line stranded onto the next page; balance each page with `\clearpage`,
  `\vspace`, and `\hspace` so each page stands on its own without overcrowding.
- Tables: every table matches the body measure exactly at `\textwidth`, every
  column is `>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}` (left aligned, ragged
  right, no large inter-word gaps), referenced with `\autoref`.
- Figures: the colored TikZ Mermaid figures are refined from the full stage
  (cleaner routing, consistent palette, captions that explain rather than
  narrate), kept strictly to black, grayscales, `#EBCB8B`, `#D08770`, `#8B2E3F`.
- Symbols: every section reference uses "§", never "SS"; single hyphens only (no
  en, em, double, or triple dashes); straight quotes consistent with the template.
- Prose: definitions precede first use; ideas connect across sections; each
  confidence question closes by pointing the clinician at the specific mechanism
  (gate, provision, or record) that answers it; the recurring questions resolve
  into a clear adoption decision.

**Sources.** Same evidence base as the full stage
(`review/references/author_works.bib`, `adoption/references/framework_refs.bib`,
the verification evidence carried in the prior review, the Bill
DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20619762), now cited cleanly with the `ieeetr` style and
`\nocite{*}` so every entry renders and each DOI prints its clickable resolver URL.

One commit each for `main.tex`, the `.sty`, `references.bib`, and `README.md`; one
commit per `sections/*.tex` file; plus `prompt-final-framework.md` and
`output-final-framework.md`. For the second-to-last commit fix all errors across
all files; for the last commit write the directory README and the
`final-framework-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle and refresh the pull request. The
manuscript must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX and preserve the template visual
theme. After this stage, the single v0.2.0 release update follows (root
`CHANGELOG.md`, `releases.md`, and the main `README.md` with its added version
section, badges, ASCII and colored Mermaid diagrams, and repository structure),
without rewriting the prior v0.1.0 content. Commit and push in real time; do not
hold commits. Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode.
